company_summary: 兆易创新 (603986.SH) is a Chinese fabless IC design company specializing in memory (NOR flash, SLC NAND), microcontrollers, sensors, and analog chips. The company benefits from the memory upcycle driven by AI demand and supply discipline. Recent performance: Q1 2026 revenue 4.19B CNY, net profit 1.46B CNY; FY2025 revenue 9.20B CNY, net profit 1.65B CNY; H1 2026 guidance revenue ~11.5B CNY, net profit ~6.9B CNY (YoY +791%).

segments.products_or_services: Memory (NOR flash, SLC NAND), microcontrollers, sensors, analog chips
segments.business_model: High R&D technology product type, fabless IC design (集成电路设计)
segments.causal_chain: AI and industrial demand driving volume and pricing, supply constraints in memory lead to sharp margin expansion, MCU and analog benefit from recovery and content growth
counterevidence: Market-implied PE TTM (105.5x) versus H1 annualized run-rate (~22x) suggests skepticism on sustainability

known_kpe.evidence: 【东吴传媒海外 张良卫团队】存... 【东吴传媒海外 张良卫团队】存储涨价预期再强化，重视板块回调机会！ 事件：7月14日，美股存储板块整体反弹，其中美光、闪迪涨幅约5%，海力士ADR收涨27%，资金重新回流存储芯片主线。本轮上涨主要催化为KeyBanc在亚洲供应链调研后大幅上调DRAM、NAND价格及美光盈利预期，进一步强化了市场对存储供给紧张延续至2027年的判断。SK海力士ADR的大涨则叠加了流通盘稀缺和杠杆ETF产品的因素。 观点重申：看懂供需，穿越周期。AI驱动存储需求高速增长，叠加供给侧寡头格局与资本开支纪律，本轮供需紧缺的持续性与价格涨幅远超市场对"传统周期"的定价。我们认为海外存储龙头正处于"周期股→成长股"的估值范式切换起点，据我们测算AI拉动下存储供不应求格局有望延续数年，存储价格有望持续维持高位。存储厂利润释放确定性强，坚定看好存储投资机会！ [礼物] 相关标的： 1）SK Hynix (SK海力士) 2）Micron (美光科技) 3）Samsung Electronics (三星电子) 4）Sandisk (闪迪) 5） 两倍做多海力士（7709.HK） 6） 两倍做多闪迪（SNXX） 7） 两倍做多三星电子（7747.HK） 8） Western Digital（西部数据） 9） Seagate（希捷科技） 10）兆易创新（603986.SH） 11）普冉股份（688766.SH） 风险提示：上游 DRAM 及 HBM 产能释放超预期风险；云厂商 AI 资本开支节奏放缓风险。

known_kpe.evidence: ‼️【中报预告+AI硬件景气：... ‼️【中报预告+AI硬件景气：谁是下一个江波龙？】——山西证券 江波龙打出高度后，市场要问的不再是能不能追，而是扩散方向在哪。我们的判断——AI产业真正缺的不只是GPU，而是一整套硬件系统：存储决定吞吐、光模块决定通信、高端PCB决定互联、先进封装决定集成、上游材料决定成本边界。订单、价格、稼动率同步改善的环节，中报预告就是新催化！ 1）存储：涨价周期叠加AI终端与服务器需求，利润弹性最容易被预告验证。重点看德明利、佰维存储、香农芯创、兆易创新、北京君正、普冉股份； 2）先进封装/Chiplet：华为韬定律V2强化后摩尔时代系统级优化，A股映射最清晰的是晶圆级封装、TSV、多层堆叠、封测与封装材料。重点看长电科技、通富微电、华天科技、晶方科技、甬矽电子、华海诚科、深科技。 3）光模块：集群越大，瓶颈越从单卡性能转向网络通信，800G/1.6T/CPO/硅光是业绩兑现方向。重点看新易盛、中际旭创、天孚通信、光迅科技、华工科技、太辰光、剑桥科技、东山精密、联特科技。 4）高端PCB/AI服务器材料：弹性不及存储，确定性更强，容易走趋势。重点看沪电股份、胜宏科技、深南电路、生益科技、生益电子、景旺电子。 5）液冷：Rubin平台单GPU功耗2300W、单机柜200kW、强制全液冷——渗透率30%→60%的核心催化剂，平台出货节奏直接决定26H2液冷链订单可见度。重点看英维克、高澜股份、申菱环境、中石科技、思泉新材。 ☎️ 下周不要被低位轮动带偏，真正的主线只有一条：中报预告+AI硬件景气度！

KPE04.evidence: 【DW电子】国产算力周思考——... 【DW电子】国产算力周思考——WAIC见闻 本周五开始的为期四天的WAIC大会上AI大厂云集，周五场已是人满为患，周末更是有过之而无不及。AI热潮并未受到交易面的影响。 我们重点走访了超节点相关的展台，包括CSP厂商（阿里、百度等）、OEM厂商（中兴、浪潮等）、各家GPU厂商。＃超节点成为本场WAIC大会各家展台C位最亮眼的坐标。 其中最显眼的当数中兴展台前展出的“超节点服务器”，上方明确列出＃“交换：中兴、博通、盛科、云合”。部分GPU厂商也有透露出Switch方面推进国产化的动作。我们依然强调＃盛科是Switch环节卡位最好、最纯粹的国产替代标的。 我们和OEM厂商访谈下来，普遍还是反馈当前超节点机柜的“标准化”还是尚未解决的问题，超节点中的各类硬件仍然是耦合度非常高的。 周五美股AI企稳反弹是一个很好的信号📶，我们依然看好国产AI算力接下来全面景气！看好反弹窗口进攻方向——国产算力[拳头] 🧧持续推荐【国产算力五大天王】寒武纪 海光信息 ｜ 芯原股份 ｜ 盛科通信 ｜ 兆易创新，建议关注晶圆制造+先进封装+测试等诸多产业环节。 恳请今年大力支持东吴电子！祝周末愉快！

KPE05.evidence: 【DW电子】每日观点：Q2预告... 【DW电子】每日观点：Q2预告小结 🔥PCB：AI驱动量价齐升，PCB产业链中报业绩全面高增 📌PCB板块中，沪电、深南、生益电子净利润同增约73%、62%、110%，Q2环增约35%、59%、49%；CCL板块生益科技、金安、南亚同增约125%、1000%、427%，Q2环增约76%、184%、107%。上游电子布宏和预告同增322%，产业链景气度传导顺畅。 📍展望下半年，PCB1.6T模块、Rubin、ASIC起量，新增产能陆续释放，业绩有望加速成长，建议特别关注PCB载板化方向上的深南电路。CCL板块建议聚焦关注M8/M9材料放量，同时关注上游电子布、铜箔等原材料环节在高端产品占比提升及加工费上涨背景下的盈利弹性。 🔥国产算力：Q2目前市场预期仍存在一定方差，但普遍环比Q1有一定改善。 📍国产算力板块没有发预告，但市场交易的点也不是在Q2业绩，我们看好国产链条26H2随着晶圆代工、封测等环节逐步拉通，有望逐季释放业绩，尤其是纯国产代工侧的业绩。我们看好26H2国产算力业绩主升浪。近期密集参加展会，产业普遍乐观，而且产业链的沟通交流以及上下游的耦合程度很高，下半年尤其要重视H链公司！ 🔥存储：利基存储涨价开始显现在业绩端，是“此前已涨价，涨价完整放映至Q2业绩”的 📌兆易创新Q2营收约73亿，归母净利润约54亿，扣非归母约35亿。扣非归母净利率达47%。江波龙Q2归母净利润53.38亿元至71.38亿元，环比增长38.22%至84.83%。德明利Q2利润环比略有下降。利基存储价格持续上涨，紧缺程度与涨价幅度MLC＞SLC＞利基DDR＞nor 📍海外原厂持续收缩利基存储产能，供给紧缺态势有望持续至27年。涨价&供给紧缺态势有望持续至27年底。Q2业绩披露后，利基存储明年利润逐渐清晰，考虑量不减/价增。至少可以按Q4利润×4线性外推 🔥模拟：工业/通信需求持续复苏，AI需求贡献增量；8英寸成熟产能满产。 📍海外TI已完成4轮涨价，覆盖面从中小客户扩大至所有客户，英飞凌、MPS大举跟进。模拟公司虽未披露预告，但Q2国内成熟代工端也同步涨价，景气程度仅次于存储。模拟料号中，我们核心看好Drmos大单品后续涨价趋势。Drmos龙头认准杰华特！目标1500e！ 🔥功率：工业需求好转叠加EV去库尾声，板块Q2业绩呈现边际改善 📌士兰微Q2归母净利润约3.10亿元，环比增长48.6%；斯达半导Q2营收约10.63亿元，环比增长23% 📍成本上涨正逐步向产品价格传导，随着调价落地，叠加800VDC方案加速渗透，看好相关公司后续业绩弹性。 🔥端侧：端侧板块二季度整体业绩表现亮眼，存储涨价对AIoT业务影响可控 📌瑞芯微26H1营收28.70-29.10亿元，yoy+40.28%-42.24%，归母净利润8.50-9.10亿元，yo...

KPE06.evidence: ‼️【中报预告+AI硬件景气：... ‼️【中报预告+AI硬件景气：谁是下一个江波龙？】——山西证券 江波龙打出高度后，市场要问的不再是能不能追，而是扩散方向在哪。我们的判断——AI产业真正缺的不只是GPU，而是一整套硬件系统：存储决定吞吐、光模块决定通信、高端PCB决定互联、先进封装决定集成、上游材料决定成本边界。订单、价格、稼动率同步改善的环节，中报预告就是新催化！ 1）存储：涨价周期叠加AI终端与服务器需求，利润弹性最容易被预告验证。重点看德明利、佰维存储、香农芯创、兆易创新、北京君正、普冉股份； 2）先进封装/Chiplet：华为韬定律V2强化后摩尔时代系统级优化，A股映射最清晰的是晶圆级封装、TSV、多层堆叠、封测与封装材料。重点看长电科技、通富微电、华天科技、晶方科技、甬矽电子、华海诚科、深科技。 3）光模块：集群越大，瓶颈越从单卡性能转向网络通信，800G/1.6T/CPO/硅光是业绩兑现方向。重点看新易盛、中际旭创、天孚通信、光迅科技、华工科技、太辰光、剑桥科技、东山精密、联特科技。 4）高端PCB/AI服务器材料：弹性不及存储，确定性更强，容易走趋势。重点看沪电股份、胜宏科技、深南电路、生益科技、生益电子、景旺电子。 5）液冷：Rubin平台单GPU功耗2300W、单机柜200kW、强制全液冷——渗透率30%→60%的核心催化剂，平台出货节奏直接决定26H2液冷链订单可见度。重点看英维克、高澜股份、申菱环境、中石科技、思泉新材。 ☎️ 下周不要被低位轮动带偏，真正的主线只有一条：中报预告+AI硬件景气度！

company_model.business_archetype: Fabless IC design: designs NOR/NAND/DRAM memory, MCU, sensors, analog chips; outsources manufacturing; sells to ODM/OEM and distributors globally.
company_model.value_proposition_and_customers: Provides critical semiconductor components for industrial, automotive, consumer, AI/cloud, IoT, and networking. Customers include module makers, device OEMs, ODMs, and distributors with growing China localization demand.
company_model.revenue_equation: Revenue = Σ(segment volume × ASP). Volume depends on design wins, foundry wafer allocation, and end-demand. ASP driven by memory cycle (NOR, SLC NAND, DRAM), product mix shift to higher-value SKUs, and ASP for MCU/analog.
company_model.profit_equation: Gross profit = revenue – wafer costs – packaging/test costs – other COGS. Operating profit = gross profit – R&D (~17% of revenue) – SG&A. Non-operating items may include fair value changes on investments; need to isolate recurring net profit.
company_model.cash_flow_equation: OCF = net profit + D&A – working capital changes (inventory, receivables). Fabless model requires low capex; major reinvestment is R&D expense (already in P&L). OCF/net profit ratio historically >1x, indicating high cash conversion.
company_model.capital_intensity_and_reinvestment: Capital-light: fabless design house with moderate R&D expense. Revenue growth requires working capital build (inventory), but asset base is mainly cash, financial investments, and some tangible assets for test/design. Reinvestment is R&D for next-gen products and customer customization.
company_model.share_count_period: 2026-07-20; latest stock_basic; Tushare stock_basic.reg_capital (CNY 10,000) / 100; CNY 1 par value
moat_mechanisms: IP and design know-how in NOR flash, MCU, and sensors accumulated over years.
Broad product portfolio (memory+MCU+sensors+analog) enabling integrated platform solutions and cross-selling.
Customer stickiness due to qualification cycles, software ecosystem (MCU SDK), and customized chip designs.
Scale in NOR flash (top-tier global supplier) providing cost advantages and foundry relationship leverage.
moat_to_financial_transmission: IP and design wins → premium ASP and higher gross margins vs generic commodity.
Platform approach → lower customer churn and more predictable revenue.
Qualification/design-in → switching cost for industrial/auto customers, supporting revenue resilience.
Scale in NOR → better wafer pricing and cost absorption, sustaining margins in down cycles.
structural_risks: Memory price cyclicality: NOR/NAND/DRAM ASP can decline sharply if supply exceeds demand.
Dependence on external foundry capacity (TSMC, SMIC, etc.); supply constraints could limit volume.
Intense competition from Winbond, Macronix, Puya, and IDM incumbents; Chinese peers ramping similar products.
Trade restrictions and geopolitical risk could disrupt wafer supply or export markets.
Technology obsolescence: new memory technologies (e.g., MRAM, ReRAM) or advanced logic embedded NVM could displace NOR.
Equity incentive dilution and potential share count overhang.
key_unknowns: Product-level revenue and margin for memory, MCU, sensors, analog in FY2025 and 2026E.
Exact non-recurring composition of H1 2026 net profit and sustainable recurring profit run-rate.
Diluted share count including stock options, restricted shares, and potential convertible instruments.
Foundry wafer allocation and capacity outlook for 2026-2028.
ASP trajectory for specific products (NOR, SLC NAND, DRAM) and duration of upcycle.
Capex and R&D capitalisation policy; free cash flow generation.
ChangXin Technology IPO stake value and ultimate financial treatment (FVOCI, no income impact, but asset value).

underwriting_questions.question: What is the exact split of H1 2026 parent net profit of 6900 million CNY between recurring core earnings and non-recurring items (e.g., investment gains, asset sales, fair value changes)?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Directly determines the sustainable earning power used for forward P/E valuation. If a large portion is one-off, the quality of earnings is lower and the market may re-rate the stock downward once the composition is disclosed.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Unresolved: sell-side estimates suggest recurring net profit ~3500 mn vs total 6900 mn, implying 3400 mn non-recurring; company claims no material undisclosed non-recurring items, but the conflict indicates note-level disclosure required.
bull_evidence: Company statements via investor interaction say ChangXin IPO fair value changes have no direct impact on net profit (FVOCI classification). (EV investor_interaction)
Management reiterated in risk-reminder announcement that guidance is based on actual operating data; no undisclosed large one-offs implied.
bear_evidence: DW electronics channel check estimates Q2 net profit 5400 mn but recurring only 3500 mn, a ~1900 mn delta; that suggests large non-operating gains. (KPE05)
Past financial reports have included fair value gains from investments; a large mark-to-market on other equity holdings could inflate net profit.
missing_evidence: Detailed income statement for H1 2026, including non-recurring items and segment-level operating vs non-operating classification
underwriting_questions.next_verification: 2026 semi-annual report (expected Aug 2026) and management's explanation of non-recurring items.

underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Memory ASP is the primary driver of revenue growth and gross margin expansion. If the cycle peaks in 2H 2026 and then normalizes, the current high earnings may not be sustainable.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Unverified proxies: TrendForce forecasts SLC NAND price +120-170% in H2 2026; multiple KPEs mention tight supply and price hikes, but no company-specific realized ASP data. Q1 gross margin 57% suggests significant ASP increase already captured.
bull_evidence: KeyBanc raised DRAM/NAND price forecasts after Asia supply chain survey; supply tight through 2027. (KPE01)
TrendForce estimates SLC NAND 120-170% increase in H2 2026. (KPE03)
Q1 2026 gross margin expansion of 19.6pp YoY implies strong ASP increases already feeding through. (EV013)
bear_evidence: Sell-side report mentions 'MLC > SLC > niche DDR > NOR' in price strength order, suggesting NOR could be last to benefit and less dramatic. (KPE05)
Historical memory cycles have seen rapid price normalization when new capacity comes online.
missing_evidence: Company-specific realized ASP per product category (NOR, NAND, DRAM) quarterly
underwriting_questions.next_verification: Quarterly data from supply chain trackers (TrendForce, IC Insights) and Q2 2026 semi-annual report segment disclosure.

underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Without sufficient capacity, even strong orders cannot translate into volume and revenue. The fabless model's volume ceiling is determined by foundry allocation.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: No disclosed capacity figures. Analysts assume company can secure incremental capacity from foundries, but this is unverified.
bull_evidence: Company management claims to maintain strong relationships with foundry partners; has historically increased supply. (EV030)

bear_evidence: Foundry capacity on mature nodes is limited; many chip designers compete for the same lines; allocation may be insufficient to meet surging demand.

missing_evidence: Foundry wafer allocation agreements, capacity outlook statements from company or foundry partners

underwriting_questions.question: What is the capex and R&D spending plan for 2026-2028, and what free cash flow can be generated after sustaining investment?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Even if net profit is high, heavy capex or R&D capitalisation could consume cash and reduce FCF, affecting dividend capacity and intrinsic value.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: No capex breakdown given. Fabless model implies low tangible capex; R&D is expensed, not capitalized, so FCF should be high in the upcycle. Historical R&D ~17% of revenue. But confirmation needed.
bull_evidence: Historical OCF/net profit ratio of 1.22x in Q1 2026 indicates strong cash conversion; low capex typical for fabless. (EV017)
bear_evidence: Rapid revenue growth may require working capital investment (inventory, receivables) reducing FCF.
If company invests in testing or R&D facilities, capex could rise.

Management guidance on capex and R&D capital allocation

underwriting_questions.question: How sustainable is the current gross margin above 55% beyond 2026, especially if the memory cycle turns or competitors add capacity?
underwriting_questions.why_it_matters: Long-term valuation multiple depends on normalized margins, not peak-cycle figures. If the market already prices in peak margins, any sign of normalization could trigger de-rating.
underwriting_questions.current_answer: Unclear. Gross margin 57% in Q1 2026 vs 37% in FY2025; some of this is cyclical ASP spike, but product mix shift and higher NOR/DRAM value-add may create a higher floor. However, history shows that margins revert when supply catches up.
bull_evidence: Company's increasing focus on high-density NOR, automotive-grade, and customized memory with longer design-in cycles could sustain a structural margin uplift.
AI edge needs for high-reliability NOR may create a sticky premium tier.
bear_evidence: Memory industry has a history of boom-bust cycles; gross margins of 60%+ attract new entry and capacity, eventually leading to price wars.
Market-implied PE 105x on TTM suggests expectation of rapid normalization, not sustained high margins.
missing_evidence: Industry capacity addition plans from competitors (Winbond, Macronix, Puya) and foundry capex
Company's product mix breakdown and high-margin product share targets
underwriting_questions.next_verification: Monitor industry supply announcements; review management's long-term margin guidance during H1 2026 briefing.

moat_evidence_tests.moat_mechanism: IP and design know-how in NOR flash and MCU
moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: Gross margin premium vs. peer (e.g., Puya, Macronix) on similar products; sustained over multiple cycles.
moat_evidence_tests.comparison_basis: Peers: Winbond, Macronix, Puya; compare reported gross margins and ASP realizations.
moat_evidence_tests.evidence_result: Partially evident: Q1 2026 gross margin 57% is high, but lacking peer comparison data for same period.
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: Higher sustained gross margin → higher and less cyclical earnings → premium P/E.
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: In previous downcycle (2023), gross margin fell to ~37%, similar to peers, suggesting any premium is cycle-dependent rather than structural.
moat_evidence_tests.next_verification: Collect peer gross margin data for 2024-2026 to compare across cycles.

moat_evidence_tests.moat_mechanism: Customer switching costs due to qualification and ecosystem lock-in (MCU SDK)
moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: Customer retention rate or average engagement length; high proportion of repeat design wins.
moat_evidence_tests.comparison_basis: Company's own track record and MCU competitor stickiness (ST, NXP, etc.)
moat_evidence_tests.evidence_result: Limited evidence; no public data on retention. Management commentary mentions platform ecosystem, but no measurable churn data.
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: High retention leads to revenue predictability and fends off price competition, supporting margin.
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: In MCU market, Chinese customers often switch to lower-cost domestic suppliers rapidly, undermining ecosystem lock-in.
moat_evidence_tests.next_verification: Customer survey or disclosed design-win repeat rates from IR.

moat_evidence_tests.moat_mechanism: Scale in NOR flash providing cost advantage and foundry relationship leverage
moat_evidence_tests.observable_test: Cost per bit lower than smaller peers; consistent unit cost trend.
moat_evidence_tests.comparison_basis: Competitors Macronix, Puya; compare income statement COGS/shipments.
moat_evidence_tests.financial_transmission: Lower cost → higher gross margin at same ASP → better profitability in down cycles.
moat_evidence_tests.strongest_counterevidence: Puya, a Chinese peer, is rapidly scaling and may match cost structure, eroding scale advantage.

valuation_buckets.valuation_method: P/E on adjusted 2026E net profit (base case recurring)
valuation_buckets.metric_and_period: Adjusted net profit 2026E: 12,000 mn (recurring portion, base case estimate)
valuation_buckets.double_counting_treatment: Excludes non-recurring items and non-operating assets; will be summed with ChangXin stake optionality.

valuation_buckets.valuation_method: 1.8% of ChangXin's expected market cap post-IPO, with haircut for lock-up and FVOCI classification (no income recognition)
valuation_buckets.metric_and_period: ChangXin IPO price reference: 8.66 CNY/share per KPE02, but total valuation unknown. Assuming valuation of ~50 bn CNY, stake ~900 mn CNY, extremely small relative to market cap.
valuation_buckets.double_counting_treatment: This asset is already on the balance sheet as FVOCI, but its movements do not affect net profit; its inclusion is as optionality of value realization upon disposal or dividend. No double-count with earnings core because earnings exclude its fair value changes.
